title: pogostemon manipurensis
intro: Pogostemon manipurensis, also known as Manipuri patchouli, is a plant species native to the Manipur region in northeastern India. It is a perennial herb belonging to the family Lamiaceae and is highly valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Pogostemon manipurensis include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
